retry_reason: On failure (ENOMEM), the original acl has already been released, so the caller no longer has a valid input to retry with; any retry would require a fresh acl object.
contract_summary: This wrapper takes ownership of the input *acl, releases the original reference, and attempts to create a clone with modified mode via posix_acl_create_masq. On success, *acl is replaced with a new acl (acquiring a new reference) that the caller must later release with posix_acl_release. On allocation failure (ENOMEM), the original is still released and *acl is set to NULL; the caller must check the return value and handle the error appropriately. The caller should not use the original pointer after calling this function.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to replace an acl reference with a newly cloned and modified one, managing the release of the original and the creation of a new reference. The masq call is a non-trivial transformation but is part of the creation semantics; refcount management is the core structural logic.

check.reason: The code calls posix_acl_release(*acl) which decrements the refcount. The access path '*acl.a_refcount.refs' and type chain 'posix_acl--refcount_t--atomic_t' are consistent. No set operation is directly performed in this function.
